flashcards.front: What does HDFS stand for and what is its role?
flashcards.back: Hadoop Distributed File System. It is the scalable, fault-tolerant storage layer of Hadoop designed to hold vast amounts of data across commodity hardware.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between the NameNode and the DataNode in HDFS?
flashcards.back: The NameNode is the master server that manages the file system namespace and metadata. DataNodes are slave servers that store the actual data blocks and serve read/write requests.

flashcards.front: What is the key limitation of using core Hadoop (MapReduce) for IoT data analysis?
flashcards.back: High latency. MapReduce is designed for batch processing and is not suitable for real-time or low-latency analytics on live data streams.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of YARN in the Hadoop architecture?
flashcards.back: YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator) is the resource management and job scheduling layer. It allocates cluster resources (CPU, memory) to various applications running on Hadoop.

flashcards.front: Give an example of a Map and a Reduce function for IoT sensor data.
flashcards.back: Map: Input `(sensor123, 2023-10-27T10:00:00, 25.7)`, output `(sensor123, 25.7)`. Reduce: Input `(sensor123, [25.7, 26.1, 24.9])`, output `(sensor123, 25.57)` (the average).
